diseases.name: Atrial Fibrillation
diseases.geneticRiskDescription: Your heart has a genetic tendency to follow an irregular rhythm. Your risk is above the average for this disease, but here's the exciting part: this is only the starting line, not the finish line!
diseases.activationDescription: Right now these 'beat-skipping' genes are more awake than asleep (78% active). This simply means your lifestyle habits have huge room to shine!

diseases.name: Coronary Artery Disease
diseases.geneticRiskDescription: Genetic markers indicate a moderate-high risk for plaque accumulation. However, epigenetics can override this via high-quality fats and antioxidant-rich diet.
diseases.activationDescription: Activation level is moderate, indicating your current lipid metabolism is coping, but requires focused optimization.

diseases.name: Hypertension
diseases.geneticRiskDescription: Slightly elevated genetic risk for salt sensitivity. Your body might hold on to sodium more than most.
diseases.activationDescription: Low-moderate activation. Potassium-rich foods and daily movement are currently keeping this risk in check.

diseases.name: Heart Failure
diseases.geneticRiskDescription: Your genetic predisposition for structural heart weakness is below average. You have a robust baseline.
diseases.activationDescription: Minimal activation. Your regular exercise and sleep quality are maintaining high cardiovascular resilience.

diseases.name: Peripheral Artery Disease
diseases.geneticRiskDescription: Lower than average risk for peripheral circulatory issues. Your genetic baseline is strong.
diseases.activationDescription: Your low inflammatory load means these genetic pathways are largely dormant.

diseases.name: Type 2 Diabetes
diseases.geneticRiskDescription: Moderate genetic baseline for insulin sensitivity. You have a good foundation.
diseases.activationDescription: Low activation. Your low-glycemic habits are successfully silencing these metabolic risk genes.

diseases.name: Genetic Obesity
diseases.geneticRiskDescription: Your genes for satiety and fat storage are near the population average. No major genetic hurdles here.
diseases.activationDescription: Activation is minimal, suggesting your caloric balance and protein intake are highly optimized.

diseases.name: Liver Steatosis
diseases.geneticRiskDescription: Very low genetic risk for non-alcoholic liver fat accumulation. You have high hepatic resilience.
diseases.activationDescription: These pathways are almost completely asleep due to your high fiber intake.

diseases.name: Metabolic Syndrome
diseases.geneticRiskDescription: Moderate genetic risk for clustering of metabolic conditions. Requires consistent monitoring.
diseases.activationDescription: Low-moderate activation. Your current lifestyle is effectively managing these predispositions.

diseases.name: PCOS Risk
diseases.geneticRiskDescription: Strong genetic protection against hormonal metabolic imbalances.
diseases.activationDescription: Activation is at basal levels. Your endocrine health is excellent.

diseases.name: Alzheimer's Disease
diseases.geneticRiskDescription: You carry markers that correlate with faster cognitive decline if left unmanaged.
diseases.activationDescription: Moderate activation. Deep sleep and brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF) stimulation through exercise are critical now.

diseases.name: Parkinson's Disease
diseases.geneticRiskDescription: Genetic risk for dopaminergic neuron degeneration is significantly below average.
diseases.activationDescription: Your low environmental toxin exposure is keeping these pathways dormant.

diseases.name: Multiple Sclerosis
diseases.geneticRiskDescription: Average genetic risk for neuro-immune conditions. No specific alarm, but worth observing.
diseases.activationDescription: Moderate activation. Managing systemic inflammation and Vitamin D levels is key to cooling these genes.

diseases.name: Ischemic Stroke
diseases.geneticRiskDescription: Moderate genetic tendency for vascular-neuro events. Strong interaction with blood pressure markers.
diseases.activationDescription: Current activation is stable. Consistent omega-3 intake is helping maintain neurovascular integrity.

diseases.name: Chronic Migraine
diseases.geneticRiskDescription: Highly sensitive genetic neurological profile. Your brain reacts strongly to environmental triggers.
diseases.activationDescription: High activation. Stress-melting activities and hydration are the 'remote control' to turn this volume down.

diseases.name: Breast Cancer
diseases.geneticRiskDescription: Genetic baseline for cell repair and tumor suppression is exceptionally strong in this area.
diseases.activationDescription: Low activation. Your cellular health is optimal.

diseases.name: Prostate Cancer
diseases.geneticRiskDescription: Genetic indicators for oncological risk here are significantly lower than population average.
diseases.activationDescription: Almost no epigenetic activation in these risk pathways.

diseases.name: Colorectal Cancer
diseases.geneticRiskDescription: Genetic foundation for gut mucosal integrity and immune surveillance is robust.
diseases.activationDescription: Very low activation. Your diverse microbiome is a key protective factor here.

diseases.name: Melanoma
diseases.geneticRiskDescription: Moderate genetic sensitivity to UV radiation and DNA damage repair speed.
diseases.activationDescription: Moderate activation. Increasing antioxidant-rich greens can help cool these sensitive pathways.

diseases.name: Lung Cancer
diseases.geneticRiskDescription: Genetic risk for pulmonary cell mutation is very low. High detoxification resilience.
diseases.activationDescription: Low activation. Your focus on air quality and non-exposure is paying off.
